steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence already has a finite main verb: "offers" ("Such a compensatory measure ___ the organelle's decline... offers a unique explanation"). The subject "a compensatory measure" drives "offers." The blank must add a modifier describing the measure without creating a second finite verb, which would cause a comma splice.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'counteracting': a present participle, which functions as a modifier. "A compensatory measure counteracting the organelle's decline" is a noun phrase with one main verb ("offers") — grammatically correct. ✓
  • Choice B — 'counteracted': simple past tense — a finite verb. This creates two finite verbs ("counteracted" and "offers") joined only by commas, making a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — 'had counteracted': past perfect — also a finite verb, creating the same comma splice problem. ✗
  • Choice D — 'counteracts': simple present tense — a finite verb. Same comma splice issue; two independent clauses cannot be joined by commas alone. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
